publications.editedComment: Fifty-five little points share the burrow. Each starts with a random position, slow two-axis drift, radius, and opacity. The frame loop clears the canvas, moves every node, bounces it from the canvas edges, and draws it. Then each unique pair is measured; points within 160 pixels receive a pale line that fades as the distance grows. Resize follows the window. The fixed canvas covers the viewport without taking clicks. Unmount cancels the animation frame and removes its listener. A tidy night sky: no mystery physics, just bounded motion and neighbor lines.

There are snacks for the quiet trail and a burst for clicking the map. Ambient hearts spawn every forty-two frames while the list stays below thirty-eight; some arrive as small clusters. Each heart has randomized position, upward and sideways velocity, lifespan, wobble, hue, rotation, size, and solid, outline, or shimmer rendering. A window click creates six to twelve forced hearts plus sixteen sparkles at that coordinate. Frames fade, move, rotate, and remove expired particles. The pointer-transparent canvas listens through the window instead. Resize, click, and animation cleanup all happen on unmount. A trail of affection, packed with its own cleanup bag.

Affection is rendered here as a population with limits. Ambient hearts enter from below the viewport, drift upward with lateral wobble, rotate, scale in, and fade near the end of randomized lifespans. Their palette spans red, rose, pink, coral, lavender, and peach, with solid, outline, and shimmer forms. Clicking anywhere creates a radial heart burst and sixteen decaying sparkles while the canvas itself remains unable to intercept the click. Expired hearts and sparkles are spliced from their arrays. The animation frame and window listeners are removed when the effect leaves. Even delight should clean its litter.

Ten wishes, all bubbles! On mount, the Component creates ten blobs with sizes from sixty to one hundred fifty pixels, horizontal positions inside the viewport, colors cycling through a ten-color list, durations from eight to eighteen seconds, negative delays, and random side swings. `blobStyle` turns those values into dimensions, position, color, animation timing, and a CSS variable. An SVG blur and color matrix merge nearby circles into goo. The infinite `lava-rise` keyframes carry each blob from below the page to 130vh above it while changing horizontal offset and scale. No click spell, no store, just calculated fizz.

My friend, the favorable conditions are fixed at ten blobs. Each receives a random size, position, travel duration, negative delay, and horizontal swing, while its color follows a ten-entry palette. The style helper writes those values directly onto circular elements. A hidden SVG filter blurs them and increases alpha contrast so nearby forms merge into a single goo layer. The `lava-rise` animation moves them from below the viewport through four height and scale stages to well above it, repeating forever. A splendid forecast while conditions hold; there is no interactive control or persistent state to overpromise.

Click: one hundred squares break from a ten-by-ten grid around the pointer. Their palette first samples visible canvas pixels, then computed foreground and background colors from nearby DOM elements, and finally falls back to six evenly spaced hues. Every square receives jittered color, size, outward velocity, upward lift, rotation, decay, and shimmer; two shockwave rings accompany the burst. Frames add gravity, resistance, spin decay, shrinking, highlights, shadows, and removal at zero life. The canvas ignores pointer events while a window listener observes clicks after underlying controls receive them. Resize, click, and animation cleanup are explicit. Thirty-one distractions; one exact explosion.

Each click lights a bounded shatter instead of blocking the page beneath it. The Component samples colors at and around the click from its canvas and nearby computed DOM styles; if too few are found, it builds a six-color HSL fallback. A ten-by-ten pixel grid then bursts outward with color jitter, randomized speed, rotation, size, decay, gravity, drag, and shaded square edges. Two expanding shockwaves mark the center. Particles and rings are removed as life reaches zero. The full-screen canvas is pointer-transparent, and the window click and resize listeners plus animation frame are removed on unmount. Bright fragments, accounted for.

The card correctly withholds the achievement artwork. It shows only the supplied icon—or a map fallback—the achievement label, and its subtle hint. A question icon toggles the full tooltip. Opening the tooltip clears any prior timer, reveals the text with tooltip and live-region roles, and schedules automatic closure after 1.2 seconds; clicking again closes it immediately. The Component accepts one Achievement prop and owns no store mutation or earning logic. One correction remains visible in the markup: `aria-expanded` is static rather than bound to `revealTooltip`. The narrative purpose is precise—tease an unearned bean without granting it.

Bosun’s inspection: the unearned card keeps prize art below deck. It renders the Achievement’s icon or a map fallback, label, and subtle hint. The question control is keyboard-focusable and toggles the supplied tooltip. Any existing hide timer is cleared before the state changes; an opened tooltip closes itself after 1.2 seconds. The card does not mark the achievement earned, call a store, or navigate away. Tooltip text uses `role=tooltip` and an aria-live region, though the control’s `aria-expanded` remains hard-coded false instead of tracking the reveal state. A useful locked chest, with one accessibility label to tighten.
